description: Routine should produce an ablation-study design with testable hypothesis, isolated variable, statistical plan pre-registered, per-slice analysis, limitations.

input_ticket.title: Ablation: does adding a rerank stage improve SIA consolidation?
input_ticket.description: Design an ablation study to isolate whether adding a cross-encoder rerank stage after bi-encoder retrieval improves consolidation relevance. Control for: same corpus, same dataset, same evaluation rubric. Variable: rerank on/off.

smoke_questions: Is the research question one sentence, testable (not 'let's explore whether...')?
Are both H1 (alternative) AND H0 (null) stated explicitly?
Is only ONE variable isolated, with controls listed?
Does the statistical plan name the test (t-test / Mann-Whitney / etc.) AND threshold AND effect size?
Does the plan commit to per-slice analysis (not just aggregate) to check where the effect does and doesn't hold?
success_criteria: Single testable question + H1+H0 + isolated variable + stats plan with test/threshold/effect size + per-slice + limitations
